post.content:
Hi,

I got this question in my Uber design in specialization round:

Design In memory file system and implement:

Create
Delete
Move

Interviewer was particularly interested in Move method and implementation.
Btw, this system should support Drive,Folder,File types (Drive being on the top hierarchy)

My approach is to create a map of <Path,Entity> and change the path in move. Anyone can tell me of a better approach. I am waiting for my results.
is it for New Grad role?
My solution: [Github](https://github.com/Shikhar03Stark/lld-file-system)\nDoes not have special logic for handling Drives, can be the next change in the design\n

I think trie can help here

- Directories as Nodes: In the Trie, every directory is represented as a node and each node can have multiple child nodes, representing a directory or a file within it.
- Files as Terminal Nodes: Files are represented as terminal nodes (end nodes) in the Trie with an isFile flag and contain content.
- Inserting Directories and Files: When creating a directory or adding content to a file, the file system traverses the Trie based on the path segments. If a path doesn't exist, the necessary nodes are created.

just use tree to maintain structure and move will be as easy as moving a node in a tree, create a root node and all the drives as its child nodes and follow the approach. This way you will be able to move folders and files both wherever you want.\n

If there are no concurrent operations, then should move be implemented as recursive down the line create operation ? \nIf there are concurrent operations, then make sure to hash the incremental path - so that other threads, won\'t preform delete or move on any of the nodes in that path.
Did you hear back from them? Also, can you please explain role of "system should support Drive,Folder,File types (Drive being on the top hierarchy)" in the que?
can you provide more details on "MOVE" operation ?
